'''Gareth "Gaz" O'Brien''' (born 31 October 1991) is an English professional [[rugby league]] footballer who plays as a {{rlp|fb|sh}} or {{rlp|so}} for the [[Leigh Centurions|Leigh Leopards]] in the [[Super League]].

He previously played for the [[Warrington Wolves]] and the [[Salford Red Devils]] in the [[Super League]], and on loan from [[Warrington Wolves|Warrington]] at the [[Widnes Vikings]], [[Castleford Tigers]] and [[St Helens R.F.C.|St Helens]] in the Super League, the [[Swinton Lions]] in the [[2013 RFL Championship|Championship]] and the [[North Wales Crusaders]] in [[RFL League 1|League 1]]. O'Brien has also played for the [[Toronto Wolfpack]] in the [[RFL Championship|Championship]] and the [[Super League|top flight]], and on loan from [[Toronto Wolfpack|Toronto]] at [[Castleford Tigers|Castleford]] in [[Super League XXV]]. He later joined the [[Castleford Tigers|Tigers]] on a permanent deal.

==Background==
O'Brien was born in [[Warrington]], [[Cheshire, England]]. He is of [[Irish people|Irish]] descent.

He is a product of the Warrington academy; he was seen as the long-term replacement for [[Lee Briers]].

O'Brien is the ambassador for the [[Rett syndrome]] research trust [https://www.reverserett.org.uk Reverse Rett], even posing as Mr. July in their Natural Wire Calendar, in which several players posed nude.

== Club career ==
===Warrington Wolves===
In the [[2011 Warrington Wolves season|2011 Season]], O'Brien made his début in the 80-0 [[Challenge Cup]] win over [[Keighley Cougars|Keighley]]; he marked his début with a try. He made his [[Super League]] début in Round 14 against [[Castleford Tigers|Castleford]], partnering [[Lee Briers]] as the half-backs. O'Brien steered the team to a 62–0 win, crossing the line for a try himself. He then featured in the record-breaking 112–0 home victory over the [[Swinton Lions]] in the 4th round of the [[Challenge Cup]]. An injury prevented him from playing for the rest of the season.{{citation needed|date=March 2023}}
[[File:GARETH O'BRIEN.jpg|thumb|right|O'Brien playing for Warrington in 2012]]
In the [[2012 Warrington Wolves Season|2012 Season]], O'Brien featured in Round 10 ([[Widnes Vikings|Widnes]]) then in Round 12 ([[Castleford Tigers|Castleford]]) to Round 20 ([[Catalans Dragons|Catalans]]) then in Round 22 ([[Bradford Bulls|Bradford]]). He also featured in Round 4 and 5 and the quarter-final of the [[Challenge Cup]] against [[Keighley Cougars|Keighley]], [[Bradford Bulls|Bradford]] and [[Catalans Dragons|Catalans]]. He scored against [[Salford Red Devils|Salford]] (8 goals), [[Widnes Vikings|Widnes]] (10 goals), [[Hull Kingston Rovers|Hull KR]] (1 try), [[Leeds Rhinos|Leeds]] (2 tries, 1 goal), [[Hull F.C.]] (6 goals), [[Catalans Dragons|Catalans]] (1 goal) and [[Bradford Bulls|Bradford]] (2 goals).

In the [[2013 Warrington Wolves Season|2013 Season]], O'Brien featured in Round 7 ([[Salford Red Devils|Salford]]) and Round 9 ([[Widnes Vikings|Widnes]]). He featured in Round 5 of the [[Challenge Cup]] against [[Keighley Cougars|Keighley]]. He scored against [[Salford Red Devils|Salford]] (1 goal), [[Widnes Vikings|Widnes]] (3 goals) and [[Keighley Cougars|Keighley]] (1 try).

In the [[2014 Warrington Wolves season|2014 Season]], O'Brien featured in Round 1 ([[St Helens R.F.C.|St. Helens]]) to Round 6 ([[Wigan Warriors|Wigan]]) then in Round 9 ([[Widnes Vikings|Widnes]]) to Round 11 ([[Bradford Bulls]]) and in Round 18 ([[Bradford Bulls]]) to Round 27 ([[Wigan Warriors|Wigan]]). O'Brien played in Round 5 ([[Doncaster R.L.F.C.|Doncaster]]) of the [[Challenge Cup]]. He also featured in the playoffs in the Elimination Play Off ([[Widnes Vikings|Widnes]]) to the Qualifying Semi-final ([[Castleford Tigers|Wigan]]). He scored against [[Widnes Vikings|Widnes]] (1 try), [[Bradford Bulls]] (2 tries), [[Castleford Tigers|Castleford]] (2 tries), [[Huddersfield Giants|Huddersfield]] (1 try, 4 goals) and [[St Helens R.F.C.|St. Helens]] (1 try, 1 goal).

In the [[2015 Warrington Wolves season|2015 Season]], O'Brien featured in the [[2015 World Club Series|World Club Series]] against [[St George Illawarra Dragons]]. He scored against the Dragons (1 try, 2 goals). O'Brien featured in Round 1 ([[Salford Red Devils|Salford]]) to Round 9 ([[Castleford Tigers|Castleford]]). He played in Round 17 ([[Catalans Dragons]]) to Round 18 ([[Huddersfield Giants|Huddersfield]]) then in Round 20 ([[Wigan Warriors]]) to Round 23 ([[Castleford Tigers]]). O'Brien also featured in the [[Challenge Cup]] in Round 6 ([[Dewsbury Rams|Dewsbury]]) to the semi-final ([[Hull Kingston Rovers]]). He scored against [[Hull F.C.]] (1 goal, 1 drop goal), [[Catalans Dragons]] (6 goals), [[St Helens R.F.C.|St. Helens]] (6 goals), [[Huddersfield Giants|Huddersfield]] (2 tries, 4 goals, 1 drop goal), [[Widnes Vikings|Widnes]] (1 goal), [[Castleford Tigers|Castleford]] (1 try, 5 goals), [[Leigh Centurions]] (4 goals), [[Wigan Warriors]] (1 try, 2 goals, 1 drop goal), [[Wakefield Trinity|Wakefield Trinity Wildcats]] (6 goals) and [[Hull Kingston Rovers]] (3 goals).

O'Brien was released by Warrington at the end of the 2015 season, alongside teammates [[Joel Monaghan]] and [[Roy Asotasi]], as the club continue their on and off-field changes.<ref>{{Cite news|date=2015-09-16|title=Joel Monaghan: Warrington Wolves let six go, including captain|work=BBC Sport|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/34273030|access-date=2020-09-05}}</ref>

====Widnes Vikings (loan)====

In March 2012, O'Brien was sent out on loan for a month to [[Super League]] side [[Widnes Vikings]].<ref>{{Cite news|date=2012-03-05|title=Warrington's Gareth O'Brien moves to Widnes Vikings on loan|work=BBC Sport|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/17258204|access-date=2020-09-05}}</ref> He made his début in Round 6 against [[Wigan Warriors|Wigan]] where he kicked 6 goals to help the Chemics to a 37–36 victory. He played in Round 7 against [[Hull F.C.]] but could only manage a goal as Widnes slipped to a 58–10 defeat. He featured in Round 8 ([[London Broncos]]) and kicked 7 goals to help Widnes to a 38–30 win. O'Brien converted Widnes' only try in the 76–6 loss against [[Catalans Dragons]] in Round 9.

==== Swinton Lions (dual registration) ====
In 2013 and 2014, O'Brien played for the [[Swinton Lions]] in the [[RFL Championship|Championship]] through their dual registration arrangement with Warrington. He made 7 appearances and scored 6 goals for the Lions in 2013, and made a further 8 appearances and scored 2 tries, 34 goals and 2 drop goals for the club in 2014.{{citation needed|date=October 2022}}

==== Castleford Tigers (loan) ====
In February 2013, O'Brien joined the [[Castleford Tigers]] on a one-month loan deal.<ref>{{Cite news|date=2013-02-14|title=Castleford take Warrington's O'Brien on loan as stop-gap for banned Rangi Chase|work=Sky Sports|url=https://www.skysports.com/rugby-league/news/13995/8499898/Castleford-take-Warrington-s-O-Brien-on-loan-as-stop-gap-for-banned-Rangi-Chase|access-date=2020-09-05}}</ref> He was signed as cover for Tigers half-back [[Rangi Chase]], who was serving a suspension.<ref>{{Cite news|date=2013-02-14|title=Castleford Tigers sign Gareth O'Brien as Rangi Chase cover|work=BBC Sport|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/21457802|access-date=2020-09-05}}</ref> He made his début in Round 3 against the [[Bradford Bulls]], and then featured in Round 4 against the [[Catalans Dragons]] when his final-moment drop goal earned a draw for Castleford.<ref>{{Cite news|date=2013-02-24|title=Super League: Castleford 17-17 Catalan Dragons|work=BBC Sport|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/21511954|access-date=2020-09-05}}</ref> O'Brien's time at Castleford was cut short when he was recalled by Warrington following an injury to half-back [[Lee Briers]].<ref>{{Cite news|last=Taylor|first=Christopher Terris|date=2013-03-13|title=Warrington Wolves recall stand off Gareth O'Brien from loan spell at Castleford Tigers|work=The Warrington Guardian|url=https://www.warringtonguardian.co.uk/sport/10285810.warrington-wolves-recall-stand-off-gareth-obrien-from-loan-spell-at-castleford-tigers/}}</ref>

====St Helens (loan)====
In May 2013, O'Brien joined [[St Helens R.F.C.|St. Helens]] on a one-month loan deal.<ref>{{Cite news|date=2013-05-30|title=St Helens sign Gareth O'Brien on loan from Warrington|work=BBC Sport|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/22715370|access-date=2020-09-05}}</ref> He made his début against [[Huddersfield Giants|Huddersfield]], and kicked 2 goals in the 25–16 loss. He then featured against [[Bradford Bulls]] (5 goals). O'Brien then kicked 8 goals against the [[Salford Red Devils|Salford City Reds]], and 1 goal against [[Hull Kingston Rovers]]. He then kicked 6 goals in the 40–24 victory over the [[Castleford Tigers]]. O'Brien helped the Saints to a famous 22–16 victory over arch rivals [[Wigan Warriors]] by kicking 2 goals. He also kicked a goal in the 26–6 victory over the [[Catalans Dragons]].

==== North Wales Crusaders (dual registration) ====
In May 2015, O'Brien played for the [[North Wales Crusaders]] in [[RFL League 1|League 1]] through their dual registration arrangement with Warrington. He made 2 appearances and scored 1 try.{{citation needed|date=October 2022}}

===Salford Red Devils===
O'Brien joined the Salford Red Devils for the 2016 season and was assigned squad number 14.<ref>{{Cite web|last=Shaw|first=Matthew|date=2015-12-13|title=Salford release squad numbers|url=https://www.totalrl.com/salford-release-squad-numbers/|access-date=2020-09-05|website=Total Rugby League}}</ref> In the [[Million Pound Game]] against [[Hull Kingston Rovers|Hull KR]], he scored an incredible near-halfway drop goal in golden-point extra time to secure Salford's place in [[Super League]].<ref>{{Cite news|date=2016-10-01|title=Million Pound Game: Hull KR 18-19 Salford Red Devils|work=BBC Sport|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/37488691|access-date=2020-09-05}}</ref> In his first season with the club, he made 32 appearances and scored 6 tries, 87 goals and 1 drop goal.

On 1 March 2017 he signed a new three-year deal with Salford.<ref>{{Cite news|date=2017-03-01|title=Gareth O'Brien: Salford Red Devils full-back signs new three-year deal|work=BBC Sport|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/39132358|access-date=2020-09-05}}</ref> In the 2017 season, O'Brien made 29 appearances and scored 9 tries, 47 goals and 3 drop goals. On 12 March 2018, having made 4 appearances and scored 1 try and 9 goals in the 2018 season, it was announced that O'Brien would leave Salford to join [[Toronto Wolfpack]] with immediate effect.<ref name=":0">{{Cite news|date=2018-03-12|title=Gareth O'Brien: Salford Red Devils full-back to join Toronto Wolfpack|work=BBC Sport|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/43377922|access-date=2020-09-05}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web|last=Darbyshire|first=Drew|date=2018-03-12|title=Salford ace Gareth O'Brien joins Toronto|url=https://www.loverugbyleague.com/post/salford-ace-gareth-obrien-joins-toronto/|access-date=2020-09-05|website=Love Rugby League}}</ref>

===Toronto Wolfpack===
O'Brien joined [[Toronto Wolfpack]] in the [[RFL Championship|Championship]] for an undisclosed fee on 12 March 2018.<ref name=":0" /> He said he "was really impressed" by the club and that joining the Wolfpack was an opportunity he couldn't afford to turn down.<ref>{{Cite web|last=Shaw|first=Matthew|date=2018-03-19|title=Gareth O'Brien breaks silence on Salford exit|url=https://www.totalrl.com/gareth-obrien-breaks-silence-on-salford-exit/|access-date=2020-09-05|website=Total Rugby League}}</ref> He made his début against [[Rochdale Hornets|Rochdale]] in a 17–18 victory. In his first season at the club, he made 24 appearances and scored 12 tries, 68 goals and 2 drop goals.

In the 2019 season, O'Brien made 27 appearances and scored 20 tries and 99 goals. This included 2 conversions in the Championship play-off final victory over [[Featherstone Rovers]], to win promotion to Super League.<ref>{{Cite news|last=Williams|first=Adam|date=2019-10-05|title=Championship play-off final: Toronto Wolfpack 24-6 Featherstone Rovers|work=BBC Sport|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/49920006|access-date=2020-09-05}}</ref>

O'Brien had made 5 appearances and scored 1 try and 7 goals for Toronto when the 2020 season was suspended due to the [[COVID-19 pandemic]]. After Toronto were forced to withdraw from the [[Super League XXV|2020 competition]], players were allowed to find new clubs for the rest of the season so he joined [[Castleford Tigers|Castleford]] on loan.

==== Castleford Tigers (loan) ====
On 10 August 2020, [[Castleford Tigers]] announced the signing of O'Brien on a loan deal for the remainder of the 2020 season.<ref>{{Cite news|date=2020-08-10|title=Gareth O'Brien: Toronto Wolfpack loan full-back to Castleford Tigers|work=BBC Sport|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/53725880|access-date=2020-09-05}}</ref> He arrived as a direct replacement for full-back [[Jordan Rankin]] who had been released during the season suspension to allow him to return to Australia.<ref>{{Cite web|date=2020-08-10|title=O'Brien joins Tigers|url=https://castlefordtigers.com/article.php?id=7001|access-date=2020-09-05|website=Castleford Tigers}}</ref> Head coach Daryl Powell likened his abilities to those of Castleford fan favourite [[Luke Dorn]]: "he can see things very quickly, his communication is great, he has got pace and his decision-making is excellent."<ref>{{Cite news|last=Craven|first=Dave|date=2020-08-15|title=St Helens v Castleford Tigers - Gareth O'Brien can be the heir apparent|work=The Yorkshire Post|url=https://www.yorkshirepost.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/st-helens-v-castleford-tigers-gareth-obrien-can-be-heir-apparent-2943324|access-date=2020-09-05}}</ref>

O'Brien made 8 appearances throughout the remainder of the season, playing at fullback and scrum-half. He scored 3 tries, 7 goals and 1 drop goal in this time.{{citation needed|date=October 2022}}

=== Castleford Tigers ===
Castleford announced the permanent signing of O'Brien on a three-year deal on 15 November 2020, after Toronto were denied readmission to Super League.<ref>{{Cite news|date=2020-11-15|title=Gareth O'Brien: Castleford sign ex-Warrington, Salford and Toronto full-back on three-year deal|work=BBC Sport|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/54952729|access-date=2021-05-06}}</ref> His addition provided tough competition for the full-back and half-back positions occupied by [[Niall Evalds]], [[Jacob Trueman|Jake Trueman]] and [[Danny Richardson (rugby league)|Danny Richardson]] - O'Brien said, "I'll be putting everyone on their toes, it's healthy and helps the competition that a squad needs. I'm looking forward to it."<ref>{{Cite web|date=2020-11-15|title=O'Brien signs three-year deal with the Tigers|url=https://castlefordtigers.com/article.php?id=7153|access-date=2021-05-06|website=Castleford Tigers}}</ref>
O'Brien was originally assigned squad number 16 for 2021, but he requested that this was changed to number 31 for personal reasons prior to the beginning of the season.<ref>{{Cite web|date=2021-03-16|title=O'Brien to wear #31|url=https://castlefordtigers.com/article.php?id=7301|access-date=2021-05-06|website=Castleford Tigers}}</ref> In Castleford's round 3 [[Challenge Cup]] match against [[Hull Kingston Rovers|Hull KR]], O'Brien kicked a 99th-minute drop goal to secure a victory for the Tigers in the fourth period of golden point extra time.<ref>{{Cite news|date=2021-04-09|title=Challenge Cup: Hull KR 32-33 Castleford - Gareth O'Brien drop-goal sends Tigers through|work=BBC Sport|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/56662280|access-date=2021-05-06}}</ref>
On 17 July 2021, he played for Castleford in their [[2021 Challenge Cup Final]] loss against St. Helens.<ref>{{cite news|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/57828390|title=Challenge Cup: St Helens beat Castleford 26-12 for first Wembley victory since 2008|work=BBC Sport }}</ref>

===Leigh===
On 20 October 2022, O'Brien signed a contract to join the newly promoted and rebranded [[Leigh Leopards|Leigh]] side.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.skysports.com/rugby-league/news/12204/12725625/leigh-leopards-promoted-side-unveil-rebrand-and-nine-new-signings-for-super-league-2023/|title=Leigh Leopards: Promoted side unveil rebrand and nine new signings for Super League 2023|website=www.skysports.com}}</ref>
On 12 August 2023, O'Brien played for Leigh in their 17-16 Challenge Cup final victory over Hull Kingston Rovers.<ref>{{cite news|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/66463438|title=Challenge Cup final: Hull KR 16-17 Leigh Leopards - Leigh claim first Wembley final win in 52 years|work=BBC Sport }}</ref>
O'Brien played 27 games for Leigh in the 2023 Super League season as the club finished fifth on the table and qualified for the playoffs. He played in their elimination playoff loss against Hull Kington Rovers.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://leighleopards.co.uk/leigh-leopards-2023-in-review/#:~:text=Overall%20playing%20record%3A,For%20694%2C%20Against%20580./|title=Leigh Leopards 2023 season in review|website=www.leighleopards.co.uk|date=16 February 2024 }}</ref>
O'Brien played 19 games for Leigh in the 2024 Super League season which saw the club finish fifth on the table. O'Brien played in the clubs semi-final loss to Wigan.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.skysports.com/rugby-league/live-blog/12204/13227859/super-league-semi-finals-live-wigan-face-leigh-for-grand-final-spot/|title=Super League semi-finals: Wigan Warriors cruise past Leigh Leopards- live commentary and video clips|website=www.skysports.com}}</ref>

In round 1 of the 2025 Super League season, O'Brien kicked the winning drop goal for Leigh as they defeated rivals Wigan 1-0 in golden point extra-time. The match had finished 0-0 after 80 minutes, the first time in Super League history.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/articles/c2k5w971j35o|title=Leigh beat Wigan in extra time after historic 0-0 draw|website=www.bbc.co.uk}}</ref>

== International career ==
In 2016 he was called up to the [[Ireland national rugby league team|Ireland]] squad for the [[2017 Rugby League World Cup qualification – Europe#Group B|2017 Rugby League World Cup European Pool B qualifiers]].<ref name="Warrington Wolves trio named in Ireland squad for World Cup qualifiers">{{cite web|date=24 September 2016|title=Warrington Wolves trio named in Ireland squad for World Cup qualifiers|url=http://www.warringtonguardian.co.uk/sport/14763089.Warrington_Wolves_trio_named_in_Ireland_squad_for_World_Cup_qualifiers|access-date=26 September 2016|publisher=Warrington Guardian}}</ref>
